---
title: "Duckweed coverage and snail abundance analysis"
---

## Introduction

This document analyses duckweed coverage and snail abundance under different treatments.

### Load libraries

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotrix)
library(lme4)
library(car)
library(readr)
library(ggpubr)
library(readxl)
```

### Data import

```{r data-import, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df <- read.table("data_coverage_snail.txt", sep = "\t", header = TRUE)
```

### Plot settings

```{r plot-settings, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
all_tp <- sort(unique(df$timepoint))

cols <- scale_color_manual(
  name = "Treatment",
  values = c("control" = "#0072B2", "heated" = "#D55E00"),
  labels = c("Control", "Heated")
)

pos <- position_dodge(0.3)

base_theme <- theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.margin = margin(b = -10)
  )
```

### Duckweed coverage analysis

```{r coverage-analysis, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
# Summary statistics
mdata.plot <- df %>%
  group_by(timepoint, treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    coverage_av = mean(duckweed_coverage, na.rm = TRUE),
    coverage_se = plotrix::std.error(duckweed_coverage, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(timepoint_f = factor(timepoint, levels = all_tp))

# Coverage plot
px_coverage <- ggplot(mdata.plot, aes(
  x = timepoint_f,
  y = coverage_av,
  color = treatment,
  group = treatment
)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = coverage_av - coverage_se, ymax = coverage_av + coverage_se),
    width = 0, linewidth = 0.8,
    position = pos,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = pos, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, position = pos, na.rm = TRUE) +
  cols +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Weeks", drop = FALSE) +
  labs(y = "Duckweed coverage (%)") +
  base_theme

px_coverage
```

### Coverage statistics

```{r coverage-statistics, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_model <- df %>%
  filter(!timepoint %in% c(0, 1))

model <- lmer(duckweed_coverage ~ treatment * timepoint + (1|block) + (1|pond), data = df_model)
Anova(model, test = "F")
summary(model)
```

### Snail abundance analysis

```{r snail-analysis, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
# Summary statistics
mdata.snail.time <- df %>%
  mutate(snail_num = parse_number(as.character(snail), locale = locale(decimal_mark = ","))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(timepoint), !is.na(treatment)) %>%
  group_by(timepoint, treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(snail_num)),
    snail_av = ifelse(n > 0, mean(snail_num, na.rm = TRUE), NA_real_),
    snail_se = ifelse(n > 1, plotrix::std.error(snail_num, na.rm = TRUE), NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(timepoint_f = factor(timepoint, levels = all_tp))

# Snail plot
px_snail_time <- ggplot(mdata.snail.time, aes(
  x = timepoint_f,
  y = snail_av,
  color = treatment,
  group = treatment
)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = snail_av - snail_se, ymax = snail_av + snail_se),
    width = 0, linewidth = 0.8,
    position = pos,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = pos, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, position = pos, na.rm = TRUE) +
  cols +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Weeks", drop = FALSE) +
  labs(y = "Snail abundance") +
  base_theme

px_snail_time
```

### Snail statistics

```{r snail-statistics, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_snail_model <- df %>%
  mutate(snail_num = parse_number(as.character(snail), locale = locale(decimal_mark = ","))) %>%
  filter(
    !timepoint %in% c(0,1),
    !is.na(snail_num)
  )

model_snail <- lmer(
  snail_num ~ treatment * timepoint + (1|block) + (1|pond),
  data = df_snail_model
)

Anova(model_snail, test = "F")
summary(model_snail)
```

### Correlation analysis

```{r correlation-analysis, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Correlation between duckweed and snails
model_corr <- lmer(duckweed_coverage ~ snail_num + (1|block) + (1|pond), data = df_snail_model)
Anova(model_corr, test = "F")
summary(model_corr)
```
